tiles.name: Coyoacán
tiles.funFact: Frida Kahlo's birthplace! Her famous Blue House museum attracts thousands daily!

tiles.name: Xochimilco
tiles.funFact: Ancient Aztec canals where colorful trajineras float among floating gardens!

tiles.name: Línea 1
tiles.funFact: Mexico City's first metro line, opened in 1969! Uses symbols for illiterate riders!

tiles.name: Roma Norte
tiles.funFact: Hip neighborhood filled with Art Nouveau and Art Deco architecture!

tiles.name: Condesa
tiles.funFact: Tree-lined Art Deco district built around a former horse racing track!

tiles.name: Polanco
tiles.funFact: Mexico City's Beverly Hills, home to embassies and luxury boutiques!

tiles.name: San Ángel
tiles.funFact: Colonial neighborhood with cobblestone streets and a vibrant Saturday art market!

tiles.name: CFE
tiles.funFact: Comisión Federal de Electricidad powers all of Mexico!

tiles.name: Chapultepec
tiles.funFact: One of the largest city parks in the Western Hemisphere at 686 hectares!

tiles.name: Bosque de Chapultepec
tiles.funFact: This forest park is twice the size of New York's Central Park!

tiles.name: Línea 2
tiles.funFact: The Blue Line connects major cultural sites including Bellas Artes!

tiles.name: Teotihuacán
tiles.funFact: Ancient pyramids! The Pyramid of the Sun is the 3rd largest pyramid in the world!

tiles.name: Templo Mayor
tiles.funFact: Main temple of the Aztecs, discovered accidentally in 1978 by electric workers!

tiles.name: Zócalo
tiles.funFact: One of the world's largest public squares, the heart of Mexico City since Aztec times!

tiles.name: Palacio Nacional
tiles.funFact: Home to Diego Rivera's famous murals depicting Mexican history!

tiles.name: Bellas Artes
tiles.funFact: Stunning Art Nouveau palace with a curtain made of nearly 1 million glass pieces!

tiles.name: Reforma
tiles.funFact: Grand avenue inspired by the Champs-Élysées, lined with monuments and skyscrapers!

tiles.name: Línea 3
tiles.funFact: The Olive Line serves 1.3 million passengers daily!

tiles.name: Ángel de la Independencia
tiles.funFact: Mexico's most iconic monument, celebrating independence from Spain!

tiles.name: Zona Rosa
tiles.funFact: Pink Zone! Historic LGBTQ+ friendly neighborhood with Korean and international flair!

tiles.name: Agua de México
tiles.funFact: Mexico City gets water from underground aquifers and distant mountain sources!

tiles.name: Santa Fe
tiles.funFact: Ultra-modern business district rising from a former garbage dump!

tiles.name: Museo Frida Kahlo
tiles.funFact: The Blue House where Frida lived, loved, and created her iconic art!

tiles.name: Museo Soumaya
tiles.funFact: Stunning silver building housing 66,000 artworks, including works by Rodin!

tiles.name: Antropología
tiles.funFact: World's largest anthropology museum, showcasing 3,000 years of Mexican culture!

tiles.name: Línea 12
tiles.funFact: The Golden Line, Mexico City's newest metro line!

tiles.name: Torre Latinoamericana
tiles.funFact: Built in 1956, survived major earthquakes thanks to innovative floating foundation!

tiles.name: Castillo de Chapultepec
tiles.funFact: The only royal castle in North America, former home of Emperor Maximilian!
